question_template: Is the sound pressure level of this recording at least {threshold} dB? Answer yes or no.
Does the calibrated sound pressure level exceed {threshold} dB?
Using the rule soft = SPL < 79 dB, does this sound pressure level fall in the soft region?
Is the sound pressure level above the midpoint of the nominal 70-110 dB range?
Under the rule veryloud = SPL >= 93 dB, would this sound pressure level count as veryloud?
Is the sound pressure level no greater than {threshold} dB? Answer with yes or no only.
Does the sound pressure level fall in the interval [{lower}, {upper}) dB?
Is the sound pressure level between {lower} dB and {upper} dB, inclusive of {lower} but exclusive of {upper}?
Does the sound pressure level lie in the {lower}-{upper} dB band?
Is the calibrated sound pressure level within the closed interval [{lower}, {upper}] dB?
Does this sound pressure level stay inside the nominal task range from 70 dB to 110 dB?
Using the predefined bins soft: SPL < 79, normal: 79 <= SPL < 86, loud: 86 <= SPL < 93, and veryloud: SPL >= 93, which category does the sound pressure level belong to?
answer_template: {'soft' if sound_pressure_level < 79 else 'normal' if sound_pressure_level < 86 else 'loud' if sound_pressure_level < 93 else 'veryloud'}

question_template: Map the sound pressure level to one of these options: (A) soft for SPL < 79, (B) normal for 79 <= SPL < 86, (C) loud for 86 <= SPL < 93, (D) veryloud for SPL >= 93. Return only the selected option.
answer_template: {'(A)' if sound_pressure_level < 79 else '(B)' if sound_pressure_level < 86 else '(C)' if sound_pressure_level < 93 else '(D)'}

question_template: If we collapse the sound pressure level into two coarse groups, low for SPL < 86 dB and high for SPL >= 86 dB, which group applies?
Classify the sound pressure level into three bands using this rule: below-normal for SPL < 79 dB, mid-range for 79 <= SPL < 93 dB, and extreme for SPL >= 93 dB.
answer_template: {'below-normal' if sound_pressure_level < 79 else 'mid-range' if sound_pressure_level < 93 else 'extreme'}

question_template: Place the sound pressure level into one of these four 10 dB bands: under-80, 80s, 90s, or 100-plus.
answer_template: {'under-80' if sound_pressure_level < 80 else '80s' if sound_pressure_level < 90 else '90s' if sound_pressure_level < 100 else '100-plus'}

question_template: Is the sound pressure level closer to the 79 dB soft-normal boundary or the 93 dB loud-veryloud boundary?
answer_template: {'79 dB boundary' if abs(sound_pressure_level - 79) < abs(sound_pressure_level - 93) else '93 dB boundary' if abs(sound_pressure_level - 93) < abs(sound_pressure_level - 79) else 'equally close'}

question_template: Relative to the nominal 70-110 dB range, is this sound pressure level closer to 70 dB or to 110 dB?
answer_template: {'70 dB end' if abs(sound_pressure_level - 70) < abs(sound_pressure_level - 110) else '110 dB end' if abs(sound_pressure_level - 110) < abs(sound_pressure_level - 70) else 'exactly midway'}

question_template: Which predefined sound pressure level boundary is nearest to the measured SPL: 79 dB, 86 dB, or 93 dB?
answer_template: {79 if abs(sound_pressure_level - 79) <= abs(sound_pressure_level - 86) and abs(sound_pressure_level - 79) <= abs(sound_pressure_level - 93) else 86 if abs(sound_pressure_level - 86) <= abs(sound_pressure_level - 93) else 93}

question_template: Is the sound pressure level closer to the loud threshold of 86 dB than to the soft-normal boundary of 79 dB? Answer yes or no.
How many decibels above the 70 dB baseline is the sound pressure level? Return the signed difference.
How many decibels below the 110 dB upper anchor is the sound pressure level? Return the signed difference as 110 minus SPL.
Normalize the sound pressure level onto a 0-to-1 scale using 70 dB -> 0 and 110 dB -> 1. Do not clip out-of-range values.
Express the sound pressure level as a percentage position within the nominal 70-110 dB interval, using 70 dB = 0% and 110 dB = 100%, without clipping.
Convert the sound pressure level to a linear pressure-amplitude ratio relative to the standard 20 microPascal reference. Return the multiplicative ratio only.
Return the measured sound pressure level as a compact key-value pair in the form 'SPL_dB=<value>'.
Round the sound pressure level down to the nearest 5 dB step. Return only that rounded-down value with unit.
Round the sound pressure level up to the nearest 5 dB step. Return only that rounded-up value with unit.
Round the sound pressure level to the nearest multiple of 5 dB.
Express this sound pressure level as a linear pressure-amplitude ratio relative to the 79 dB boundary. Use the formula 10^((SPL - 79) / 20). Return only the ratio.
Express this sound pressure level as an intensity ratio relative to the 86 dB boundary. Use the formula 10^((SPL - 86) / 10). Return only the ratio.
